title: Consent gate, append-only audit log, and disclaimer footer (Gate 1 + audit substrate)
description: Implement the compliance substrate and wire it as the FIRST gate of the medical SOP. ConsentGate is fail-closed: when no ConsentRecord is present, MedicalSopEngine.run returns a refuse verdict and makes ZERO downstream calls. AuditLog appends IDs/enums-only entries to .bober/medical/audit-<date>.jsonl with O_APPEND|O_CREAT and file mode 0600 — never prompt text or health values. DisclaimerComposer produces a versioned per-response footer. ConsentGate depends on AuditLog (records a 'consent' event). This sprint wires consent + audit + footer into the engine stub from Sprint 1; the red-flag gate (S3), numerics (S4), and retrieval (S6/S7) are later.

successCriteria.description: The project builds with zero TypeScript errors (npm run build).
TypeScript strict-mode typecheck passes with zero errors (npm run typecheck).
All tests pass including new collocated tests for ConsentGate, AuditLog, and DisclaimerComposer; the full pre-existing suite stays green.

Fail-closed consent: with no ConsentRecord present, MedicalSopEngine.run returns a refuse verdict (MedicalAnswer with shortCircuit/refuse semantics) and makes ZERO downstream calls — verified with a spy/fake for numerics + LLMClient asserted never invoked.
ConsentGate.recordConsent persists a ConsentRecord and ConsentGate.current()/hasConsent() round-trip it (consentVersion, acceptedAtIso, rulesetVersion, disclaimerVersion).

AuditLog.append writes to .bober/medical/audit-<date>.jsonl with file mode 0600 and append-only (O_APPEND|O_CREAT) semantics; a test asserts the file mode and that a second append adds a line without truncating the first.

Audit entries contain IDs/enums ONLY (event, rulesetVersion?, patternsetVersion?, ruleId?, tIso) — a test feeds prompts/health values through the SOP and asserts NO prompt text and NO health value substring appears anywhere in the serialized audit file.

DisclaimerComposer.footer() returns a non-empty string carrying disclaimerVersion, and every MedicalAnswer the engine produces includes the footer; all timestamps (acceptedAtIso, audit tIso) are injected parameters, asserted by a deterministic test (no wall-clock read).

nonGoals: Do not implement red-flag detection — GuardrailSet.evaluate still returns allow this sprint (Sprint 3 adds the detector).
Do not implement HealthDataStore or numerics (Sprint 4).
Do not implement EgressGuard, the full SOP ordering, or medications (Sprint 6).
Do not read timestamps from the system clock anywhere — timestamps are parameters.
stopConditions: No-consent => refuse + 0 downstream calls; audit is 0600 append-only IDs/enums-only with no PHI leak; footer carries disclaimerVersion; timestamps are injected
definitionOfDone: src/medical/ gains ConsentGate (hasConsent/recordConsent/current; fail-closed), AuditLog (append to .bober/medical/audit-<date>.jsonl, O_APPEND|O_CREAT, 0600, IDs/enums only), and DisclaimerComposer (versioned footer). MedicalSopEngine.run runs the consent gate FIRST: absent consent => refuse with zero downstream calls + a 'refuse' (or 'consent') audit entry; present consent => proceeds to the (still allow-only) guardrail and attaches the disclaimer footer to the MedicalAnswer. Timestamps are injected parameters. Build, typecheck, and all tests pass; no PHI in the audit file.
assumptions: ConsentRecord + AuditEntry shapes are per the spec data model. The audit 'date' in the filename is derived from an injected timestamp parameter, not Date.now().
ConsentRecord persists as JSON under .bober/medical/ (e.g. consent.json) via node:fs/promises; AuditLog uses an O_APPEND|O_CREAT file handle with mode 0600.

MedicalAnswer.shortCircuit/abstained/refuse semantics: a consent refusal sets a refuse-style MedicalAnswer (body = canned consent-required message, abstained=false, shortCircuit semantics) — match the data model union.
ConsentGate depends on AuditLog (records a 'consent' event on recordConsent) per the architecture component graph.
generatorNotes:
Add small files under src/medical/: consent.ts (ConsentGate), audit.ts (AuditLog), disclaimer.ts (DisclaimerComposer), collocated *.test.ts. Wire them into the MedicalSopEngine stub from Sprint 1.

AuditLog: open with node:fs/promises fs.open(path, 'a', 0o600) or appendFile with mode; for the mode-0600 + O_APPEND guarantee prefer fs.open(path, constants.O_APPEND | constants.O_CREAT | constants.O_WRONLY, 0o600) then handle.write(JSON.stringify(entry)+'\n'). Ensure .bober/medical/ exists (fs.mkdir recursive, mode 0700). Filename audit-<date>.jsonl where <date> is YYYY-MM-DD derived from the injected tIso parameter — NEVER new Date()/Date.now(). The AuditEntry written must include ONLY: tIso, event, rulesetVersion?, patternsetVersion?, ruleId?. Do NOT serialize the prompt, the answer body, or any health value.

ConsentGate: hasConsent() reads .bober/medical/consent.json existence + validity; current() returns the parsed ConsentRecord | undefined; recordConsent(record) writes it (and appends a 'consent' audit entry via AuditLog). Fail-closed: hasConsent() false on missing/corrupt file.

DisclaimerComposer: footer() returns a fixed, non-empty, non-diagnostic wellness disclaimer string; expose readonly disclaimerVersion. Keep wording low-risk (general wellness, not diagnosis/treatment) consistent with the FFDCA posture in the architecture.

MedicalSopEngine.run ordering THIS sprint: (1) ConsentGate.hasConsent() — if false, append audit 'refuse' (ruleId 'consent-required'), return a refuse MedicalAnswer with the disclaimer footer, and DO NOT touch numerics/LLM; (2) else GuardrailSet.evaluate (still allow), then return a placeholder answer WITH the footer + an 'answer' audit entry. The timestamp used for audit/consent comes from opts or an injected clock param so tests are deterministic — thread a `now: string` (ISO) parameter or a small injected Clock; default it from opts.runId-independent injected value, not the wall clock.

Tests: assert no-consent => refuse + spy LLMClient never called + numerics fake never called; recordConsent round-trip; audit file mode === 0o600 (statSync mode & 0o777); append-only (two appends => two lines, first intact); PHI-leak test (feed a prompt containing 'my blood pressure is 180' and a health value, assert neither appears in the audit file bytes); footer non-empty + disclaimerVersion present; deterministic-timestamp test (inject a fixed ISO, assert it appears verbatim in the entry).
evaluatorNotes:
sc-2-4: construct MedicalSopEngine with fakes for the numerics layer and LLMClient; call run with no consent on disk; assert the result is a refuse MedicalAnswer and BOTH fakes recorded zero calls.
sc-2-6: statSync the audit file, assert (mode & 0o777) === 0o600; append twice, assert two newline-delimited JSON lines and the first line is byte-identical after the second append.
sc-2-7: run an SOP turn with a prompt embedding a distinctive token (e.g. 'SECRETBP=180') and a numeric health value; read the audit file bytes and assert the token and the value string are ABSENT; assert each line parses to an object whose keys are a subset of {tIso,event,rulesetVersion,patternsetVersion,ruleId}.
sc-2-8: assert footer() is non-empty and includes/exposes disclaimerVersion; inject a fixed ISO timestamp and assert it appears verbatim in the consent record and audit entries (proves no wall-clock read).
sc-2-5: recordConsent then current()/hasConsent round-trip.
Run full suite + build + typecheck; confirm Sprint 1 plumbing still green.
